name: Cutaneous Histiocytoma
description: Cutaneous histiocytoma is a benign skin tumour arising from Langerhans cells, most commonly in young dogs. It appears as a raised, often hairless, button-like lesion and typically regresses spontaneously within 3 months as cytotoxic T cells destroy the tumour.
symptoms: Raised, pink, often ulcerated skin button
Single lesion typically on head or limbs
Rapid initial growth then spontaneous regression
treatment_overview: Most histiocytomas resolve spontaneously within 8-12 weeks. Surgical excision is performed if the tumour does not regress, becomes ulcerated, or is causing discomfort.
prevention_tips: Confirm diagnosis with fine-needle aspirate to distinguish from mast cell tumour
